name: Melatonin

sources.title: Melatonin: What You Need To Know (NCCIH)
PubMed review literature on melatonin indications

description: Night-time hormone available as OTC supplement in many US products (often 0.5–5 mg).
summary: Endogenous circadian hormone used for sleep and jet lag with modest short-term benefits and neuroactive caution.
mechanism: Acts at MT1/MT2 receptors to shift circadian phase and support sleep onset.
interactions: May add effects with sedatives; caution with anticoagulants and immunosuppressants.
dosage: Commonly 0.5–5 mg taken 30–60 minutes before bed.
regulatoryStatus: US dietary supplement; some countries regulate higher doses as prescription sleep medicines.
mechanisms: Acts at MT1/MT2 receptors to shift circadian phase and support sleep onset
